bids.reasoning: The negotiation has just started (t=0.0). The opponent proposed an offer consisting entirely of `valueE`, which provides the absolute minimum utility for me across all issues. To establish a strong anchor and set clear expectations, I will start by proposing my ideal outcome: `valueA` for all issues, targeting my maximum possible utility of 1.00.

The negotiation has just begun (t=0.02) and the opponent's offers provide very low utility, primarily sacrificing my most important issues (issueA, issueB). To signal flexibility without significantly compromising my interests, I will concede slightly on issueE (my least important issue) from valueA to valueB while holding firm on the more heavily weighted issues (A, B, and C) to maintain an exceptionally high overall utility.

The negotiation has just begun, but the opponent is making significant concessions on issueA and issueB, which are my highest-priority issues. Taking note of their firmness on issueC, issueD, and issueE, I will start making small concessions on my lower-priority issues (issueD and issueE) to signal cooperation, while maintaining my highly valued targets for my top priorities.

The negotiation is in the early stages (t=0.06). Evaluating the opponent's offers, they have made significant concessions on my highest-priority issues (issueA and issueB), indicating these might be of lower importance to them, while they are holding firm on issueD and issueE. I will reciprocate their movement by making a small concession on my lowest-priority issues (issueD and issueE) to valueC, while maintaining my top values for issueA, issueB, and issueC.

The opponent significantly conceded on issueA and issueB, offering me my top values for both issues, which are my highest-weight items. Since time is still very early (t=0.08), I will lock in valueA for issueA and issueB, and continue to incrementally concede on my lower-priority issues (issueC, issueD, issueE) to foster an agreement while still targeting a high total utility for myself (~0.86).

The opponent is consistently conceding on my most highly valued issues (issueA and issueB, currently offering both at valueA) while holding firm on their presumed most important issues (issueD and issueE at valueE). Since it is still extremely early in the negotiation (t=0.1) and we are making rapid progress, I can afford to concede slightly on issueD and issueE (my lowest priorities) to reciprocate their concessions, while holding firm on issueA, issueB, and preserving high value on issueC.

The opponent has made significant concessions early on, particularly by moving to my highest values (valueA) on both of my most important issues (issueA and issueB). However, they have held firm on issueD and issueE with valueE, and repeated their offer on issueC with valueC. Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.12), I will concede entirely on issueD by dropping to valueE to match their proposal, in hopes they agree to my slightly higher value on issueC (valueB). This proposal yields a strong utility of 0.80 for me.

The opponent has repeatedly offered the same bid allocating complete utility to me on issues A and B, which gives me a strong utility of 0.76. Since it is still extremely early in the negotiation (t=0.14), I will test if I can still secure valueB on issueC (giving me 0.80+ utility) instead of valueC. I will offer a very slight variation on our previous bids to signal firmness around my current stance while avoiding an exact repetition.

The opponent has conceded heavily on issueA and issueB, hitting my maximum utility for both, indicating these are their least important issues. Conversely, they are holding exceptionally firm on issueD and issueE (offering valueE), which suggests these are highly critical to them. Because issueD and issueE are my lowest-priority issues, I can concede on them to secure maximum value on issueA and issueB, while trying to negotiate issueC to valueB before relenting entirely to valueC. I will offer a compromise maintaining their preferred valueE incrementally on one of the less important issues while seeking valueB on issueC.

The opponent has significantly conceded on issues A and B, matching my highest preferences (valueA). They are holding firm on issueC (valueC) and issues D/E (valueE). Since time is still very early (t=0.18), I will maintain the agreement on issues A and B, hold slightly firmer on issueC (requesting valueB), and make marginal concessions on my lower-weighted issues (D and E) to see if we can find a mutually agreeable tradeoff before I match their issueC preference.

The opponent has repeatedly offered {"issueA": "valueA", "issueB": "valueA", "issueC": "valueC", "issueD": "valueE", "issueE": "valueE"}, signaling a strong preference for "valueC" on issueC while holding firm on "valueE" for issues D and E. The utility of their offer to me is 0.76, which is acceptable but can be improved since we are only at t=0.20. I will concede issueC to "valueC" to align with their demands on that issue, but I will counter by requesting "valueC" on issues D and E to maintain a high utility of 0.84 for myself.

The opponent has conceded completely on my most highly valued issues, issueA and issueB. Their latest offer provides a utility of 0.78, which is very solid, especially early in the negotiation (t=0.22). Since they recently shifted from valueE to valueD on issueD, I will reciprocate their latest move by accepting valueD on issueD but countering with valueC on issueE to maintain a strong utility of 0.82.

The opponent has fully conceded on issueA and issueB, which are my highest-weight issues, allowing us to focus on issueC, issueD, and issueE. They strongly insist on valueE for issueE and valueC for issueC, so I will maintain my top values for issueA and issueB while probing different mid-range combinations for the remaining issues to find an agreeable tradeoff. For this round, I'll propose valueC for issueC while keeping issueD and issueE slightly higher to maintain a utility above 0.80.

The opponent has repeatedly yielded my top preferences for issueA and issueB, which gives me immense utility. They are holding firm on valueE for issueD and issueE, and valueC for issueC. To encourage agreement without dropping my utility too quickly, I will accept their request for issueE: valueE, maintain issueC: valueC, but request valueC on issueD to explore alternate tradeoffs.

The opponent has repeatedly held firm on issueC at valueC, issueD at valueD/valueE, and issueE at valueE. Their latest offer yields a utility of 0.78 for me. Since we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.28), I will accept their proposal on the most important issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC) but continue seeking a minor concession on issueD and issueE to keep my utility above 0.80.

The opponent has consistently offered my best values on the highest-priority concerns (`issueA` and `issueB`) while anchoring on `valueC` for `issueC` and `valueE` for `issueD` and `issueE`. As we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.3), I have room to negotiate for slightly higher value in the lower-priority issues. I am maintaining the agreed values for issues A, B, and C, and proposing terms slightly above their last offer on the remaining minor issues to gauge flexibility.

The opponent is holding firm on `issueC: valueC`, `issueD: valueE`, and `issueE: valueE`, while giving me my absolute highest-value choices on my two most heavily weighted issues, `issueA` and `issueB`. Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.32), I am able to hold my utility slightly above 0.80. I will concede to their preferred `valueE` on `issueE` and `valueC` on `issueC`, but test if they are willing to make a small concession on `issueD` to reach an agreement soon.

The opponent has consistently offered me `valueA` on `issueA` and `issueB`, which are my most valuable issues, giving me excellent utility right off the bat. They seem to place high importance on `issueC`, `issueD`, and `issueE`. Since it's still early in the negotiation (t=0.34), I will gladly accept their concessions on A and B, and experiment with combinations of values on the minor issues (C, D, and E) to find a tradeoff they might find acceptable without compromising my utility too much.

The opponent has consistently targeted `valueC` for `issueC` and `valueE` for `issueE`, but at t=0.28 they showed a willingness to concede on `issueD` (offering `valueD`) when I proposed `valueC` for `issueD` and met their demands on `issueC` and `issueE`. Since it's still relatively early (t=0.36), I will return to my t=0.26 offer to re-anchor `issueD` around a higher utility value (`valueC`), securing an overall strong utility of 0.80 without giving up completely on my mid-tier issues.

The opponent has consistently offered full value on the high-priority issues A and B, maintaining a firm stance on valueC for issueC and valueE for issues D and E. This yields a very high utility for us (0.76). To find a mutually acceptable agreement while keeping our utility around 0.80, I will agree to valueC on issueC, but attempt a modest compromise on issues D and E by offering valueD to see if it brings us closer to a deal.

The opponent heavily prioritizes obtaining valueE on issues D and E, as well as valueC on issueC, while they consistently concede valueA on my most important issues, A and B. They previously offered `issueD: valueD, issueE: valueE` with `issueC: valueC` at t=0.28. I will propose this exact combination to secure a high-utility (0.78) agreement early in the mid-game.

The opponent had been consistently proposing {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueE, issueE: valueE} for several rounds before pulling back on issueC in their latest turn. Since time is still relatively early (t=0.42), I am seeking a slight concession on issueE in exchange for conceding issueD to their preferred 'valueE'. This maintains high utility (~0.78) while matching their established preference pattern for the first four issues.

The opponent has been consistently offering to give me 'valueA' on both 'issueA' and 'issueB', which are my highest-weight issues, resulting in an offer that yields a high utility of 0.76. Since it is still mid-game (t=0.44), I will accept their stance on the less important issues D and E, but attempt to secure a slight improvement on 'issueC' (from valueC to valueB) to target a utility of 0.80.

The opponent has repeatedly given me my preferred values for issueA and issueB, effectively conceding on my most heavily weighted issues. However, they are holding firm at valueC or valueD for issueC and valueE for issueD and issueE. At nearly the half-way point, I will cement the agreement on issueA and issueB while seeking small compromises on issueC, issueD, and issueE to keep my target utility above 0.80.

The opponent has fully conceded on the most important issues to me (issueA and issueB), proposing consistent offers of 'valueC' for issueC, and 'valueE' for issues D and E. As we approach the midway point (t=0.48), I will accept their preferred positions on issues A, B, C, and D, and ask for slightly more on issueE in an attempt to capture 0.80 utility without straining the agreement. This tests if they can concede on issueE while getting their demands met on issueD.

The opponent is holding extremely firm on `issueC: valueC, issueD: valueE, issueE: valueE`, occasionally varying on C and D. Fortunately, they agree on `issueA: valueA` and `issueB: valueA`, which are my highest-weight issues and give me significant utility. At t=0.5, I will offer a very slight variation of their bid to test if they can give up just a bit more utility on issueD, while matching their demands on issues C and E, maintaining a solid total utility of 0.78.

The opponent has consistently offered a bid that maximizes my two most important issues (issueA and issueB), yielding a total utility of 0.76. Since my attempts to gain marginal improvements on issueD and issueE have been repeatedly rejected and we are at the halfway mark, I will accept their standing offer to secure this excellent return.
